commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Codex app-server approval routing so local/custom model providers avoid Codex Guardian/model-backed review, preserves provider-qualified model selection across app-server flows, and exposes exec auto-review helpers through the Plugin SDK.

PR surface: Source +1247, Tests +2217. Total +3464 across 23 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source and regression tests: current main can force Codex `auto_review` for exec auto mode without provider trust checks, and the PR exercises local/custom/provider-qualified cases; I did not run a live LM Studio/Ollama path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Protected maintainer PR with compatibility, security-boundary, and SDK-contract tradeoffs; no narrow automated repair is indicated.

target.title: fix(codex): avoid guardian review for local models
target.body:
## Summary
- Downgrade Codex app-server Guardian/model-backed approval reviewers to user/OpenClaw approvals unless the effective reviewer context is clearly OpenAI.
- Treat explicit custom providers, provider-qualified local model refs, persisted binding model providers, side-question forks, and bound conversation turns as fail-closed for Guardian/model-backed review.
- Add OpenClaw internal exec auto-review for Codex app-server command approvals only for `tools.exec.mode=auto`, simple parsed command approvals, and an explicit `tools.exec.reviewer.model` under `openai/...`; unsafe control commands, amendments, security-audit suppression, unparsed commands, and reviewer ask decisions fall back to plugin/human approval.
- Refresh the Plugin SDK API baseline for the exec auto-review contract and keep current-main extension lint cleanup green.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: local/custom model Codex app-server auto approval no longer tries to use Codex Guardian/model-backed review, and internal exec auto-review requires an explicitly OpenAI-qualified reviewer model.
Real environment tested: delegated Blacksmith Testbox for focused Vitest plus changed gate; local static generated-baseline/hash and diff checks.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-server-policy.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/approval-bridge.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts src/channels/message/ingress-queue.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.path.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.script-preflight.test.ts`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --shell -- "set -euo pipefail; env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=true corepack pnpm check:changed"`.
Evidence after fix: focused Testbox Vitest `tbx_01kt0ecje3j7b0a3ycdyvd1qrg` passed 3 shards: 98 agent tests passed / 4 skipped, 289 Codex extension tests passed, and 8 channel tests passed; final autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings; final Testbox `tbx_01kt0fb4jc9q1efbgf3090zs9n` passed `check:changed` with exit 0.
Observed result after fix: policy, config, side-question, run-attempt, conversation-binding, approval-bridge, ingress queue, and bash exec regressions pass; changed-gate typecheck/lint/guards pass on the rebased branch.
What was not tested: live LM Studio/Ollama app-server round trip was not run; coverage is via policy/bridge regression tests, Codex upstream source inspection, autoreview, and the changed gate.
target.author: vincentkoc
